building_name: Fairland Cannabis / Pho Ngon, Augusta Ave
facade_colour: black painted storefront framing with large red FAIRLAND channel letters above and warm wood patio structures in front of the adjacent Pho Ngon unit

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: black painted storefront framing with large red FAIRLAND channel letters above and warm wood patio structures in front of the adjacent Pho Ngon unit
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Commercial strip frontage appears recently renovated and active; glazing and painted panels are in good condition, though adjacent walls carry graffiti.
photo_observations.window_details: Large ground-floor commercial glazing divided into several bays with slim dark mullions; one bay appears temporarily covered or screened with posters or artwork.
photo_observations.door_details: Two glazed commercial entries, one centered within the Fairland storefront and one serving the adjacent Pho Ngon restaurant frontage.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Thin modern parapet and fascia with integrated lighting and oversized FAIRLAND rooftop sign.
photo_observations.porch_details: No porch; restaurant side has temporary wood patio enclosures and platforms at sidewalk edge.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney visible in the street-facing photo.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Oversized FAIRLAND letters, cannabis leaf iconography, black fascia signage, and temporary wood dining sheds along the Pho Ngon frontage.
photo_observations.storefront_description: One-storey commercial frontage with a black modernized cannabis shop facade and adjacent restaurant entry partially screened by wood patio structures.
photo_observations.signage_observed: FAIRLAND, RETAIL CANNABIS STORE, ONE LOVE ONE PLANT, OPEN, and Pho Ngon Dat Viet Vietnamese Kitchen signage.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Heavily altered contemporary storefront with replacement glazing, modern fascia, rooftop sign letters, and seasonal patio structures.
photo_observations.notes: Best view is IMG_20260315_155258507_HDR.jpg; the earlier near-Dundas image is only a partial oblique view.

_meta.address: Fairland Cannabis / Pho Ngon, Augusta Ave

storefront_description: A black modernized cannabis-shop frontage sits beside the restaurant entry to Pho Ngon, which is partially screened by warm-toned wood patio structures extending toward the sidewalk.
notes: Best view is IMG_20260315_155258507_HDR.jpg; the earlier near-Dundas image is only a partial oblique view.

storefront.description: A black modernized cannabis-shop frontage sits beside the restaurant entry to Pho Ngon, which is partially screened by warm-toned wood patio structures extending toward the sidewalk.

facade_detail.composition: The street frontage spans roughly 5 metres across 1 storeys beneath a flat roof behind low parapet, consistent with its commercial typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The visible opening pattern is organized as 5 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 2 principal entrances.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Key storefront and facade features include cornice.

facade_description: Fairland Cannabis / Pho Ngon, Augusta Ave presents a commercial street frontage within the Kensington Market HCD. The street frontage spans roughly 5 metres across 1 storeys beneath a flat roof behind low parapet, consistent with its commercial typology. The frontage is defined primarily by mixed in a black painted storefront framing with large red fairland channel letters above and warm wood patio structures in front of the adjacent pho ngon unit palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 5 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 2 principal entrances. Key storefront and facade features include cornice.
